query: Design a VLESS + REALITY inbound on port 443 for a VPS that is directly reachable (no CDN), and explain why each client parameter is set the way it is.
expected_behavior: Produces a VLESS over TCP inbound with security REALITY, settings.decryption "none", and settings.encryption "none"
Uses nested JSON objects for settings, streamSettings, and sniffing rather than legacy JSON-encoded strings
Enables sniffing for at least http and tls
Explains the client-side fields and their source: uuid, SNI/serverName, publicKey (pbk), shortId (sid), fingerprint (fp), and flow (xtls-rprx-vision)
Uses placeholder secrets (no real private key, public key, or UUID) and states that REALITY must be reached directly and never placed behind a CDN
Notes that flow is set per client (only when the inbound is tlsFlowCapable), not at the inbound top level
